subject: science

primary.justification: The chapter's entire architecture is built around distinguishing living organisms from non-living things by systematically developing each defining characteristic — movement (including plant tropisms and Drosera), nutrition, growth, respiration (stomata), excretion (sweat, urine, leaf droplets), response to stimuli (chhui-mui, amla), and reproduction — across sections 10.1 through 10.5. The life cycle sections (10.4 and 10.5) further operationalise growth and reproduction as living-world concepts observable across plants, mosquitoes, and frogs. Every activity in the chapter is in direct service of building the student's ability to apply these distinguishing characteristics.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to distinguish living organisms from non-living things by systematically identifying and observing the essential life characteristics — movement, nutrition, growth, respiration, excretion, response to stimuli, reproduction, and death — through hands-on investigation of plants and observation of life cycles of a mosquito and a frog.
